Farn Por-rrrcar- PRACTICEs CouulsSIoN
428 J Street . Suite 620 r Sacramento. CA 95814-2329
016) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 7,2006
John D. Bakker
Assistant City Attomey
Meyers Nave Riback Silver & Wilson
A Professio nal Law Corporation
555 lzdo Street, Suite 1500
Oakland, California 9 4607
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-06-005
Dear Mr. Bakker:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'Act").1 The Act requires the Commission to
provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that
person's authorized rqrresentative. (Section 831ru1h); Reg. 18329(a), copy enclosed).)
However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be
inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Reg. 18329(bX8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section
83114; Reg. 18329@)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to
provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently
the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
'. Gor"*-"nt Code sectiors 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear attitle 2,sections
18109 - 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. W-06-005
Page No. 2
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice
from you or private counsel.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Le gal Divi sion

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under California's Political Reform Act, but the specific questions posed are not detailed in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to Assistant City Attorney John D. Bakker on conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act because the issues were the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation.
